artifacts.description: Run log documenting dataset search, verification of BRFSS e-cigarette variable documentation, and failure to retrieve a usable microdata file for analysis in this environment.

limitations: I could not retrieve a usable microdata file in this run, so no prevalence estimate or association analysis was executed.
The user-provided BRFSS 2015 diabetes GitHub mirror is a reduced diabetes-focused dataset and is not an appropriate source for an e-cigarette/epilepsy study as described in the idea.
Although official CDC BRFSS 2022 documentation confirms a current e-cigarette variable exists, I did not obtain the underlying respondent-level file in this environment.
Without a retrieved analytic dataset, I could not produce tables, figures, regression estimates, or weighted prevalence calculations.
Any answer about prevalence or linkage to epilepsy would have been speculative, so I am returning no empirical findings.
methods: I translated the idea into a cross-sectional observational question and attempted to identify a public microdata source containing, at minimum, sex, age, current e-cigarette use, and epilepsy status. I searched the web for BRFSS sources and documentation, prioritizing the user-provided dataset hints and official CDC documentation. I verified from the official 2022 BRFSS documentation that a calculated current e-cigarette-use variable (_CURECI2) exists in BRFSS 2022, and I inspected the suggested BRFSS 2015 diabetes GitHub mirror conceptually via the user hint. I then attempted to retrieve a directly downloadable CSV mirror for local analysis, but the execution environment could not download the candidate raw file from raw.githubusercontent.com, and no alternative directly retrievable microdata source containing both e-cigarette use and epilepsy was successfully obtained during this run. I saved a run log documenting the search and retrieval failure.
research_question: Among U.S. women younger than 50 years, what is the prevalence of current e-cigarette use, and is current e-cigarette use associated with self-reported epilepsy in a publicly accessible survey dataset retrievable in this run?

sources.label: CDC BRFSS 2022 Survey Data and Documentation landing page
sources.notes: Official CDC page confirming availability of 2022 BRFSS documentation and downloadable materials; opened during this run.

sources.label: CDC Calculated Variables in the 2022 BRFSS Data File
sources.notes: Official PDF opened during this run; confirms _CURECI2 is the calculated current e-cigarette-use variable derived from ECIGNOW2.

sources.label: BRFSS 2015 Diabetes Health Indicators GitHub mirror referenced in user hint
sources.notes: Candidate CSV mirror suggested by the user hint. Retrieval was attempted from raw.githubusercontent.com in this run but failed due network/DNS resolution in the execution environment.

title: Feasibility assessment of estimating e-cigarette prevalence in women under 50 and its association with epilepsy
